scope.goal: Name the object and index behind the repeated auth selector comparison [R8 + RAX*4 + 0x100] == 1.

working_names.auth_controller_plus_0x16C_to_0x178: auth/controller dword exports consumed by client-script op handlers

record_guard: 0x1401F532D, 0x1401F533F, and 0x1401F5349 require three 0x20-byte records to have byte [record+0x18] == 2.
dword_stack_pop: 0x1401F5353 decrements [R8+0x10A0] after R8 is copied from the incoming RDX context.
0x1401F535A loads the post-decrement cursor into EAX.
consumer_pop_paths.auth_effect: SETZ SIL feeds a later +0xB8 selector-mode argument; popped value 1 selects selector 1, any other value selects selector 3 when the auth code is six characters.

dword_stack_pop: 0x1401F55D2 loads the post-decrement cursor into EAX.
consumer_pop_paths.auth_effect: SETZ BL is forwarded into 0x1401536B0, eventually controlling the +0xB8 selector-mode byte.

dword_stack_pop: 0x1401F5A72 loads the post-decrement cursor into EAX.
consumer_pop_paths.auth_effect: SETZ BL is forwarded into 0x1401539B0, eventually controlling the +0xB8 selector-mode byte.

push_sequence: Treats incoming RDX as the interpreter context.
Reads current depth from [R8+0xFA0], which aliases context+0x10A0.
Stores [auth_controller+0x178] into [RDX+0x100 + old_depth*4], then writes depth+1 back to context+0x10A0.
The pushed value is loaded from [auth_controller+0x174].
The pushed value is loaded from [auth_controller+0x16C].
The pushed value is loaded from [auth_controller+0x170].

registration_evidence.interpretation: These handlers are registered as client-script/interpreter operation handlers. Their job is to export auth-controller dwords into the interpreter dword stack, where later auth submission handlers pop and compare the value to 1.

relationship_to_previous_selector_mappin.source_stack: context+0x100 dword stack, indexed by the post-decrement context+0x10A0 cursor
relationship_to_previous_selector_mappin.remaining_unknown: The exact semantic names of auth_controller+0x16C/+0x170/+0x174/+0x178 still need broader writer/consumer mapping.

opennxt_947_impact: Selector 1 versus selector 3 is now tied to an interpreter/client-script dword stack value, not only a local native branch.

OpenNXT 947 auth tests should keep selector handling parameterized by a client-script-visible auth-controller dword value. A value of 1 selects selector 1; any non-1 value selects selector 3 for six-character auth codes.
The four auth-controller dword fields at +0x16C, +0x170, +0x174, and +0x178 are likely important compatibility state because client-script op handlers expose them to the auth submission path.
next_targets: Map writers and wider consumers of auth_controller+0x16C, +0x170, +0x174, and +0x178 so the exported client-script values can be named.
Map registration helper 0x1400399C0 enough to recover a native table of opcode id -> handler pointer for auth-adjacent opcodes.
Correlate these handler opcode IDs with JS5 clientscript exports to identify the scripts that choose selector 1 versus selector 3.
Use the now-named interpreter dword stack to continue resolving selector-0 +0xBC/+0xC0 flows.
